summary: McKenzie walks through the technical process of renaming a popular web page for SEO purposes, changing 'Free Resources' to 'Printable Bingo Cards.' He shows the bash commands for bulk-updating HTML files, emphasizes the critical importance of 301 redirects to preserve PageRank and user experience, and warns against over-optimizing filenames with keyword stuffing. The post is a practical tutorial wrapped around a real change he made to his own site.
key_points: Renaming pages to match search-friendly keywords (like 'printable bingo cards') is a concrete SEO tactic that costs almost nothing to implement
301 redirects are essential when renaming pages — failing to add them wastes hard-earned PageRank and breaks third-party links
There's a threshold for keyword density in URLs beyond which Google will penalize you, making over-optimization counterproductive
Always resubmit your updated sitemap to Google after making URL changes
money_quotes: If you do not do it, not only are you hurting user experience on third party sites which trusted you with a link, you're squandering PageRank that you've worked so hard to gain.
Adding in another rewrite rule takes like 5 seconds, so do it!
Repetition in the URL gives no useful information to human users, and it is not a 'natural' design technique for a website either.
I suspect that Google will eventually toss it out as a useless SEO technique, the same way they habitually ignore overly-long-file-names-stuffed-with-keywords-like-this-one.htm.
This is a fairly key step for any change you make that affects a popular page.
key_insight: When renaming web pages for SEO, the 301 redirect preserving your existing PageRank and external links is just as important as choosing the better keyword-rich URL.
